title: lathyrus digitatus var. puberulus
intro: Lathyrus digitatus var. puberulus, also known as the hairy peacock's ear or hairy sweet pea, is a perennial legume plant in the genus Lathyrus. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Lathyrus digitatus var. puberulus include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce oxidative stress, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically harvested in the summer months and can be used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
